name: Steamed Ginger Chicken with Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Ginger juice or minced ginger
1/4 cup Salted fermented black beans, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the chicken into Chinese serving pieces—about an inch and a half.
2. Use a juicer to extract the ginger juice or mince the ginger.
3. In a bowl, combine sugar, salt, cornstarch, sesame oil, soy sauce, and sherry to form a smooth paste.
4. Rinse the black beans in a strainer until visible salt is gone, then drain and mince finely with garlic.
5. Add the black beans and minced garlic to the seasoning paste and mix well.
6. Add all ingredients to the chicken and mix thoroughly to coat.
7. Place the chicken on two 8-inch pie plates and put each plate into a steamer compartment on an aluminum steamer.
8. If you don't have a steamer, improvise using a large pot and a low-sided dish held just above the water by an inverted dish or similar setup.
9. Ensure steam circulates around the chicken while keeping boiling water from splashing into the dish holding the chicken.
10. Start the water boiling and place the plates onto the inverted dish in the large pot.
11. Cover tightly and steam for 25 to 30 minutes, slightly longer if you like your chicken well done.
12. The water should be boiling just enough to produce steam.
13. Serve with steamed rice, chopped green onions, hot mustard, hoisin sauce, and Vietnamese chili dip.
14. Enjoy the rich and subtle flavors of this dish, which pairs well with the freshness of green onions and the variety of dips.
This makes enough for four servings. There won't be any leftovers. The fermented black beans, hoisin sauce, fresh ginger, and sesame oil are specialized ingredients but can usually be found at an Oriental market or supermarket. Store them in the refrigerator for extended use. The sesame oil should be the dark, cooked variety from an Oriental market, not the health store variety. Fresh preserved tea melons or sweet pickles also complement this dish well. Avoid letting the chicken sit in the bean sauce mixture too long as it is not a marinade and may become overpowering.
